equivalence_argument: No complete implementation mapping was found in the audited local repo set: Missing a CLI/0x080C delivery decoder that uses fields 6-10 and forces delivery value to zero; current shared two-phase decoder consumes field 11 as callback_value, which is the agent type field in the 0x080C ABI.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing a CLI/0x080C delivery decoder that uses fields 6-10 and forces delivery value to zero; current shared two-phase decoder consumes field 11 as callback_value, which is the agent type field in the 0x080C ABI.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-async-fees/src/lib.rs and crates/ritual-block-verification/src/lib.rs special-case SOVEREIGN_AGENT_PRECOMPILE for work-based Phase 2 fees from iterations/tool_calls.

crates/ethereum/payload/src/ritual_async.rs decodes delivery config for SOVEREIGN_AGENT_PRECOMPILE from field 6, which reads target/selector/gas/max fee/priority fee from fields 6-10 but also reads callback_value from field 11.
crates/ritual-precompile-codecs/src/sovereign_agent.rs shows field 11 is sovereign_agent_type, not a delivery value, and no from_cli_agent_abi or CLI-specific zero-value delivery decoder was found.

calculate_async_fees_phase2 rejects SOVEREIGN_AGENT_PRECOMPILE generically, with builder/verifier special-casing it instead; that pricing part is present but insufficient because the delivery value semantics are wrong.

rationale: The payload builder and verifier treat CLI Agent like agent calls for work-based pricing, the generic Phase 2 fee path explicitly excludes it, and a dedicated delivery decoder extracts CLI Agent's delivery fields from its ABI layout. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: The pricing constants are marked uncalibrated, so exact rates may change; the durable behavior is using work metrics and the CLI-specific delivery ABI rather than byte pricing or the generic long-running decoder.
statement: CLI Agent Phase 1 fees should use the same constant task-start fee path as agent calls; generic Phase 2 byte pricing should reject CLI Agent and require work-based pricing from `(iterations, tool_calls)`, while settlement delivery config for 0x080C should decode target, selector, gas limit, max fee, and priority fee from fields 6-10 and use zero delivery value.
suggested_falsification_tests: For a CLI Agent settlement result containing known iterations/tool_calls, assert builder and verifier compute the same work-based fee and reject missing metrics; assert `DeliveryConfig::from_cli_agent_abi` returns fields 6-10 with value zero and malformed selectors or token types fail decoding.
summary: Work-based 0x080C pricing is present, but CLI delivery value decoding is a confirmed gap.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Build a 0x080C delivery request with sovereign_agent_type=1 and otherwise zero callback value semantics; current decode_delivery_config_at(input, 6) will treat field 11 as a nonzero callback_value instead of forcing zero.
title: CLI Agent settlement uses work-based pricing and CLI delivery fields
